title: Gifted and Talented Students (Local)
description: This policy governs how the school district identifies, serves, and when necessary, exits students from its Gifted and Talented (GT) program, including the processes for appealing those decisions and how the program is funded. It applies to students currently enrolled in or being considered for GT services, as well as their parents and guardians. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines their rights to appeal if their child is removed from the GT program and helps them understand how the district manages and resources these specialized educational services.

timeline.summary: This page shows redline changes to a Gifted and Talented policy covering Exit Provisions, Appeals, and Funding sections. Key changes include: updating terminology from 'selection' to 'placement' committee throughout; revising exit criteria language from 'best interest of the student to exit' to 'not meeting the student's educational needs'; updating appeals language; and significantly revising the Funding section to replace a general program standards statement with specific language requiring the Superintendent (rather than 'District') to develop administrative procedures aligned with TEA financial compliance guidance, and adding a new sentence requiring 100% of state GT funds be spent on GT services or program enhancement.
changes_added: is not meeting the student's educational needs (replacing prior exit standard language)
services in (replacing 'selection for or exit from')
Superintendent or designee shall develop administrative procedures to account for the expenditure of the
allotment in alignment with the Texas Education Agency's financial compliance guidance.
One hundred percent of state funds allocated for gifted and talented education shall be spent on providing gifted and talented services or enhancing the District's gifted and talented program.
identification (replacing 'assessment' in the phrase 'provides an overview of the identification procedures and services for the program')
placement (as part of 'placement committee' — replacing 'selection committee')
changes_removed: it is in the best interest of the student to exit
program shall address effective use of funds for programs and services consistent with the standards in the state plan for gifted and talented students.
assessment (in the phrase 'provides an overview of the assessment procedures and services for the program')
selection (as part of 'selection committee' — appearing twice: once in the Selection section and once in the Transfer Students/Interdistrict section)

timeline.summary: This page contains redline changes to the gifted and talented program policy covering Exit Provisions, Appeals, and Funding sections. Key changes include: (1) updating terminology from 'selection' to 'placement' committee; (2) revising exit standard language from 'best interest of the student to exit' to 'not meeting the student's educational needs'; (3) updating appeals language from 'selection for or exit from' to 'services in'; (4) overhauling the Funding section to require the Superintendent or designee to develop administrative procedures aligned with TEA financial compliance guidance, and adding a requirement that 100% of state G/T funds be spent on G/T services or program enhancement.
changes_added: is not meeting the student's educational needs
services in the gifted and talented program (in Appeals section)

Superintendent or designee shall develop administrative procedures to account for the expenditure of the gifted and talented allotment in alignment with the Texas Education Agency's financial compliance guidance.
One hundred percent of state funds allocated for gifted and talented education shall be spent on providing gifted and talented services or enhancing the District's gifted and talented program.

placement (added before 'selection' in 'placement selection committee' in the Interdistrict section)

selection for or exit from (in Appeals section)
program shall address effective use of funds for programs and services consistent with the standards in the state plan for gifted and talented students.

selection (struck through as part of 'placement selection committee' in the Interdistrict section)

timeline.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
